basic_details.job_title: IREL Apprentices Recruitment 2026 - Apply Online for 38 Graduate, Technician, Trade Apprentice Posts
basic_details.job_category: Mini-Ratna Category-1, PSU under Department of Atomic Energy, Govt. of India
basic_details.job_location: OSCOM Unit, Matikhalo, District: Ganjam, Odisha – 761045
basic_details.short_information: IREL (India) Limited, OSCOM Unit has officially published the apprenticeship notification (No. IREL/OS/AA/2026, dated 24th April 2026) inviting applications for engagement of apprentices under the Apprentices Act, 1961. The official notification (5 pages + application form) contains complete details including trade/discipline-wise vacancy table with seat count, special notes on eligibility, selection methodology, priority categories for selection, CGPA/marks conversion norms, how to apply (online + hard copy), documents required, postal address, and the prescribed application proforma. Candida

important_dates.qualification eligibility window (gradua: Within 3 years from the date of passing the Degree/Diploma
important_dates.document verification: To be intimated to shortlisted candidates - Provisional Offer Letter sent by email

application_fee.details: The official notification does not mention any application fee for IREL OSCOM Apprentice Recruitment 2026. Registration on the NAPS Portal (https://www.apprenticeshipindia.org) and the NATS Portal (https://nats.education.gov.in) is free of cost, and the application itself is also free. | Candidates are advised to apply only through the official portals using the IREL Establishment Registration Numbers as specified - NAPS Establishment Code E0421210003 and NATS Establishment Code EORGNC000001. Do not use third-party websites or agents for applying. However, candidates must bear their own postal/courier charges for sending the hard copy application to the OSCOM unit.

age_limit.details: As on the last date of application (15th May 2026), the minimum age is 18 years and the maximum age is 25 years. Reserved category candidates are entitled to age relaxation as per Government norms. SC/ST candidates receive a relaxation of up to 5 years (maximum age: 30 years). OBC (Non-Creamy Layer) candidates receive a relaxation of up to 3 years (maximum age: 28 years). Persons with Disabilities (PwD) receive a relaxation of up to 10 years (applicable over and above the category relaxation for SC/ST/OBC). Candidates not meeting the age criteria as on the last date of application will be treated as rejected.

qualification.education_qualification: As specified for each trade/discipline in the vacancy table - only prescribed qualification accepted; higher qualification not permitted

salary_details.monthly stipend: As prescribed under Apprenticeship Rules, 1992 (revised by Govt. from time to time)
salary_details.accommodation: NOT provided - apprentices must arrange their own stay
salary_details.ta / da / other financial assistance: NOT payable during the training period
salary_details.regular employment: IREL has NO obligation to offer regular employment after apprenticeship completion
salary_details.police verification certificate: Mandatory for entry into the highly protected IREL OSCOM area - must be obtained from concerned police authorities and submitted prior to joining
salary_details.details: Selected apprentices will receive a monthly stipend as prescribed under the Apprenticeship Rules, 1992, as notified by the Government of India from time to time. The stipend rate depends on the category of apprentice (Graduate, Technician, or Trade/ITI) and is revised periodically. | IREL will not provide any other benefit or financial assistance to apprentices during the training period - no accommodation, travel allowance (TA), daily allowance (DA), or any other perquisites will be provided. Apprentices must arrange their own accommodation. IREL has no obligation to offer regular employment after completion of the apprenticeship period. Upon successful completion, candidates will be relieved from the respective work area.

selection_process: Priority Category
Priority I Candidates belonging to Land Affected Category (documentary proof of having given land to IREL required)
Priority II Candidates who are permanent residents of villages within a radius of 07 km from OSCOM Unit
Priority III Candidates who are wards of IREL employees, including candidates of employees who died while in service
Priority IV Candidates who are permanent residents of villages beyond 07 km but within a radius of 10 km from OSCOM Unit
Priority V Candidates who are domicile of Odisha State (documentary proof required)
Priority VI Candidates from Rest of India
Stage Details

Stage 1: Online Portal Registration Register on NAPS (https://www.apprenticeshipindia.org) for ITI/Trade apprentices using Establishment Code E0421210003, OR on NATS (https://nats.education.gov.in) for Graduate/Technician apprentices using Establishment Code EORGNC000001.

Stage 2: Hard Copy Application Submission Fill prescribed application proforma (printed back-to-back on A4 paper), affix passport-size photograph, attach all required self-attested documents, and send by Speed Post/Courier to reach IREL OSCOM on or before 15th May 2026.
Stage 3: Merit-based Shortlisting Based on marks in qualifying examination. Priority categories applied. Tiebreaker: higher marks → date of birth (older preferred).

Stage 4: Document Verification Shortlisted candidates called in merit order. Original documents + self-attested photocopies required. Provisional Offer Letter sent by email. Candidature rejected if originals not produced or eligibility not satisfied.
Stage 5: Medical Examination Candidates must be declared "Medically Fit" by IREL's Medical Officer. Final engagement subject to satisfactory medical fitness.

Stage 6: Police Verification Mandatory Police Verification Certificate from concerned police authorities (of area where candidate normally resides) - must be submitted prior to joining, as OSCOM is a highly protected area.
Selection will be based on merit determined by marks scored in the specified educational qualification. In case of a tie in marks
the candidate with higher marks in the qualifying examination will be preferred. If the tie persists
seniority in date of birth (older candidate preferred) will be the tiebreaker. No canvassing or influencing will be acceptable - this will result in rejection of the candidature.

Shortlisted candidates will be called for document verification in the order of merit and must bring original documents along with one set of self-attested photocopies. Final engagement is subject to satisfactory medical fitness as certified by IREL's Medical Officer. Reservation is as per the Government of Odisha guidelines. A unique priority-based selection system also applies to give preference to local
land-affected
and IREL employee-related candidates.
Priority Categories for Selection
Selection Stages
how_to_apply: The application process requires both online portal registration and submission of a physical hard copy application - both are mandatory. Applications without online portal registration will not be accepted, and online registration alone without hard copy submission is also insufficient. For ITI/Trade Apprentices, register on the NAPS Portal (https://www.apprenticeshipindia.org) using Establishment Code E0421210003. For Graduate/Technician Apprentices, register on the NATS Portal (https://nats.education.gov.in) using Establishment Code EORGNC000001.

After completing online registration, fill the prescribed application proforma (preferably type-written on A4 paper, printed back-to-back on the same sheet), affix a recent passport-size photograph, attach all required self-attested document copies, and send by post/courier (preferably Speed Post) to the address below. The application must reach IREL OSCOM on or before 15th May 2026. Applications received after this date will not be considered regardless of the reason.

Step 1: For ITI/Trade Apprentices - Register on NAPS Portal: https://www.apprenticeshipindia.org (Establishment Code: E0421210003). For Graduate/Technician Apprentices - Register on NATS Portal: https://nats.education.gov.in (Establishment Code: EORGNC000001).
Step 2: Fill the prescribed application proforma (type-written, on A4, printed back-to-back on same sheet of paper).
Step 3: Affix recent passport-size photograph in the space provided on the application form.
Step 4: Attach self-attested copies of all required documents as per the checklist.
Step 5: Super-scribe the envelope as: "APPLICATION FOR ENGAGEMENT OF APPRENTICE AGAINST NOTIFICATION NO. IREL/OS/AA/2026 - Discipline Applied for: [Trade/Discipline Name]"
Step 6: Send by Speed Post / Courier to reach the address below on or before 15th May 2026.
Postal Address: Head of Department, HRM, OSCOM, IREL (India) Limited, Matikhalo, District: Ganjam, Odisha – 761 045
important_instructions: Both online portal registration (NAPS/NATS) AND hard copy application submission are mandatory. Hard copy application must be sent by post/courier (preferably Speed Post) only - other modes not accepted. Higher qualification than prescribed = candidature cancelled. Do not suppress higher qualification information. Distance/Part-time/Correspondence mode qualifications are NOT accepted. Graduate/Technician Apprentice candidates must have passed within the last 3 years. Candidates already undergone/pursuing apprenticeship anywhere are NOT eligible. Police Verification Certificate is mandatory for entry into IREL OSCOM - obtain it in parallel after applying. No accommodation, TA, DA, or other financial assistance will be provided. Reservation as per Govt. of Odisha guidelines. Priority-based selection for local/land-affected candidates. IREL will not be responsible for postal delay - send applications well in advance. All future updates only on www.irel.co.in - visit the website periodically.

faq.question: Q1. How many apprenticeship seats are available in IREL OSCOM Apprentice Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: IREL (India) Limited, OSCOM Unit has announced a total of 38 apprenticeship seats under Notification No. IREL/OS/AA/2026 dated 24th April 2026. The seats are distributed across four categories: Graduate Apprentice (2 seats in Chemical and Instrumentation), Technician Apprentice (6 seats across Chemical, Electrical, Mechanical, Instrumentation, and Civil), Trade Apprentice for non-ITI positions (7 seats in Geologist, Laboratory, HRM, Finance, and Marketing), and Trade Apprentice ITI (22 seats across Fitter, Electrician, Painter, Plumber, Carpenter, Welder, AC Mechanic, and Solar Electrician trades).

faq.question: Q2. What is the educational qualification required for different apprentice positions?
faq.answer: Educational qualifications vary by position. For Graduate Apprentices, B.E./B.Tech in Chemical or Instrumentation/IT/CS is required. For Technician Apprentices, Diploma in the respective engineering field (Chemical, Electrical, Mechanical, Instrumentation, or Civil) is mandatory. For Trade Apprentices, specific qualifications include M.Sc./B.Sc. (Geology) for Trainee Geologist, M.Sc./B.Sc. (Chemistry) for Laboratory, MBA-HRM/MA-IRPM for HRM, MBA-Finance/ICWA (Inter)/ICAI (Inter) for Finance, and MBA-Marketing for Marketing positions. For ITI Trade Apprentices, ITI certification in the respective trade (Fitter, Electrician, Painter, Plumber, Carpenter, Welder, AC Mechanic, or Solar Electrician) is required.

faq.question: Q3. What is the age limit for IREL OSCOM Apprentice Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: The minimum age is 18 years and the maximum age is 25 years as on the last date of application (15th May 2026). Age relaxation is provided for reserved categories: SC/ST candidates receive 5 years relaxation (up to 30 years), OBC (Non-Creamy Layer) candidates receive 3 years relaxation (up to 28 years), and Persons with Disabilities (PwD) receive 10 years additional relaxation over and above their applicable category relaxation, making the maximum age 35 years for General PwD, 40 years for SC/ST PwD, and 38 years for OBC PwD candidates.

faq.question: Q4. What is the stipend for IREL OSCOM Apprentices?
faq.answer: Selected apprentices will receive a monthly stipend as prescribed under the Apprenticeship Rules, 1992, and as notified by the Government of India from time to time. According to recent IREL notifications, the approximate stipend rates are: Trade Apprentices receive ₹7,000-₹8,000 per month, Technician Apprentices receive ₹8,000-₹9,000 per month, and Graduate Apprentices receive ₹9,000-₹10,000 per month. However, IREL OSCOM will not provide any other benefits - no accommodation, travel allowance (TA), daily allowance (DA), or other financial assistance will be provided during the training period.

faq.question: Q5. How to apply for IREL OSCOM Apprentice Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: The application process requires both online portal registration AND submission of a physical hard copy application - both are mandatory. For ITI/Trade Apprentices, candidates must register on the NAPS Portal (apprenticeshipindia.org) using Establishment Code E0421210003. For Graduate/Technician Apprentices, registration on the NATS Portal (nats.education.gov.in) using Establishment Code EORGNC000001 is required. After online registration, candidates must fill the prescribed application proforma (type-written on A4 paper, printed back-to-back), affix a recent photograph, attach all self-attested documents, and send by Speed Post/Courier to reach "Head of Department, HRM, OSCOM, IREL (India) Limited, Matikhalo, District: Ganjam, Odisha – 761 045" on or before 15th May 2026.

faq.question: Q6. What is the last date to apply for IREL OSCOM Apprentice Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: The last date for receipt of hard copy applications is 15th May 2026. Applications received on or after this date will not be considered, regardless of the reason for delay. IREL will not be responsible for postal delays, so candidates are advised to send their applications well in advance, preferably by Speed Post or reliable courier service. The notification date is 24th April 2026, giving candidates approximately three weeks to complete the application process.

faq.question: Q7. What is the priority-based selection system in IREL OSCOM recruitment?
faq.answer: IREL OSCOM has implemented a unique priority-based selection system to give preference to certain categories of candidates. Priority I is given to candidates belonging to Land Affected Category (those who gave land to IREL - documentary proof required). Priority II goes to permanent residents of villages within 7 km radius from OSCOM Unit. Priority III is for wards of IREL employees, including candidates whose parent died while in service. Priority IV covers permanent residents of villages between 7-10 km radius from OSCOM Unit. Priority V applies to candidates who are domiciled in Odisha State, and Priority VI includes candidates from rest of India. Within each priority category, merit is determined by marks in the qualifying examination.

faq.question: Q8. Is there any application fee for IREL OSCOM Apprentice Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: No, there is no application fee for IREL OSCOM Apprentice Recruitment 2026. Registration on both the NAPS Portal (apprenticeshipindia.org) and NATS Portal (nats.education.gov.in) is completely free of cost. The application submission itself is also free. However, candidates must bear their own postal or courier charges for sending the hard copy application to the OSCOM unit in Odisha. Candidates are advised to apply only through the official portals using the specified IREL Establishment Codes and not use any third-party websites or agents.
